transcript.text: Last week was and it had some in-person sessions.
Harmony was mentioning.
Yeah, we did some pre-planning last week, which is nice.
Planning week is officially done tomorrow.
So it's good.
Sweet.
How are you guys?
We're growing fast over here.
Have someone wanted to introduce to the gang here.
have Stuart just joined us this week as a managing director.
Comes with a bunch of SEO experience.
Excited to add some more strategy support for Elvis and his accounts.
And so I'm thinking to him as somewhat playing a similar role as myself, but giving us even more capacity experience and ideas that we can help supercharge what we're doing.
Nice to meet everyone.
Happy to be here.
Me too.
Hi guys.
Welcome, welcome.
All right.
Maybe Stuart, can do like...
Quick round of intros on the other side so you have a sense of who we are.
I lead.
We're great.
Thank you.
I lead the product marketing team here at Supreme.
Popcorn, it's Morgan.
I am on Harmony's team and I lead our DemandGen programs.
Harmony.
Hi, I'm Harmony.
I lead growth and marketing.
Very excited to have you here.
Nice meeting you all.
Thank you for the intros.
Awesome.
Well, let's kick into the business as usual.
Lots to go through.
Elvis.
Not a whole lot, but let's just start, shall we?
So just an update of where we are with the content.
Starting to ramp up.
We implemented image generation like we talked about the other week.
So that's all up and running.
Everything is kind of flowing very well.
We published our first kind of batch of content.
End of last week.
Going into this week as well.
planning to have 20 plus more ready to go for this week to publish.
And in the coming weeks, just scaling up and up.
In terms of, you know, I generally don't usually like to show the analytics early, but, you know, at least it's going in the right direction.
So already getting a whole bunch of impressions.
This is just two of the articles.
Others are kind of still indexing.
So that's OK.
But, hey, good early signs.
Just for context, you know, I think we might have talked about this during the kickoff, but, you know, I have the luxury of kind of seeing all our accounts and typically what we see.
So early, you know, early publishing days, we're looking to see that impression grows typically.
Like, course, hey, if we can get a bunch of clicks from day one, amazing, but that tends to be less typical, especially when it is starting to ramp up content.
A place where not a lot of the content has been produced recently.
And so we expect us to continue to compound as long as we can continue the right pace of like continuing to publish content and give that signal like this is something that Google can now expect.
And so as best as we can keep that momentum, this will help us here.
And so what we'll start to see typically in the coming weeks is faster indexation.
These pages will start to see these clicks compound typically around like week three, four ish after publishing, we'll start to see the clicks start to increase steadily.
It's usually and this is it can vary a little bit by month three or so of like really being consistent about early month three of consistency of publishing content moving fast.
So we'll start to see like those first like big upticks in clicks typically and like that 200 plus a week range.
Now this isn't like universally true because like every client like the keywords are going after.
So search volume, but usually it's somewhere in the range of like month two, you'll be seeing 20 to 50-ish clicks a week.
And then by early month three, that's what we expect to see that like 200 plus a week start to end compound from there.
Sometimes that trajectory can be a little bit different, but the key point here just to note is like as best as now that we're published, keep momentum with developing a content and publishing.
We should be able to maintain pace with that velocity.
Of course, we're always going to be exploring different ways we can change that and not, you know, try to drive results to ASAP.
But that's the typical average.
Thanks, Kyle.
I couldn't have said it better myself, even if I wanted to.
Oh, I'm sure you could have.
I don't think so.
All right.
That was good.
Before we get into Alex, your comments on the shared reporting.
Uh-huh.

A couple weeks back, we talked about maybe like, I want to, I'd like to put a file together where we can have your guys' like GIFs that you use in articles and images and arcade, whether that's even implementable within articles, because right now it's fine, but I'm just pulling from certain articles, but you know, I can't, it's tough to like look through like a hundred and find the right one.
And so I'm just kind of trying to find what's out there, what's relevant.
So it'd be great to, and I'm not saying like everything, just the key stuff, like a split inbox.
If we can just have like, I don't know, 20 to 30 different widgets and elements, however you want to call them and like have a file, would that be something you guys could put together?
Yeah, absolutely.
A lot of, I mean, all this stuff exists.
It's just making a repository that's easy for y'all to use.
It's, you know, there's like.
Three arcades today and as we publish new ones we can kind of share those.
What's your take on like YouTube videos or are those not that applicable?
Would we rather have GIFs?
Great.
YouTube is great if it's relevant.
I'd have to look through your YouTube catalog again because you guys have quite a few videos on there, right?
Yeah, like there's a lot of like product launch animation type things that I think are really good, like, you know, one minute walkthroughs on different like feature sets and things like that.
So that's a good one.
And then there is a GIF library on Dropbox, which I can share.
The caveat is that like, they're from different eras of Superhuman until like the design is not like super consistent.
It's something that we're going to try and address in Q2.
But yeah, if you want to make just a page in our shared notion doc of this and I can like link to all of the things, just like ping me on Slack when it's created and I can, I can work.
With my team to kind of pull together at least like the best set of assets that we have as a starting point.
Yeah, totally.
And it'd obviously be better just to have the most current stuff.
I don't want to use old stuff.
We just want like the newer version.
Yeah, that's a little bit.
I have to like ask my team a couple of questions about some of the things.
Like we still use some of the old ones in like our emails and whatnot too.
So it's not like we're not using them to the company, but I have it on our list to update the like hero feature versions of those if there are outdated this quarter.
All right, Kyle.
Do you all have a content calendar for upcoming videos?
Upcoming videos?
Not really.
We have like a customer moments calendar, which I'm trying to finalize in the next like day or so.
Cause we're in the middle of our quarterly planning.
It's like, what are our big campaigns?
Marquee.
Project launches, webinars, and things like that.
So when that's finalized, we can share it with you.
I can give you a quick snapshot here if it's helpful.
But we're still trying to figure out exactly which of the marquee moments are going to have videos and some of those things.
But I should have a clearer sense by next week for sure.
Okay, cool.
Yeah.
I'm just trying to see if there's some angles.
I think I've mentioned this a little bit before, but different ways we can kind of just plug in into your existing strategies and ways you may not be thinking about.
You know, like we talked about the report last week, which is great.
But, you know, we have another client, like, for example, here, if I could just share my screen real quick.
I'll just give you a little bit of a flavor, kind of what I'm thinking.
Of course, my internet is going to start crapping out right now.
Sorry, give me one second.
I don't know why everything's loading so slowly all of a sudden.
Here we go.
This is a client of ours, Galileo.
Oh, they spun up a podcast back in the fall.
It is a weekly podcast.
And what we pitched them on was basically, we take the transcript from this podcast.
And it's not just a beautifying of the transcript.
It's creating sections that tell a story related to what's in there.
It pulls quotes out, things of that nature.
So the way we would leverage this here, perhaps with you all, may be a little bit different, but just trying to tease a little bit of what's possible.
Because they're already doing this.
They were going to do this anyways.
And then we took this and we said, hey, we can start to create SEO-rich articles off of this that hopefully can drive traffic through.

But also, you know, as they're doing things like webinars, what I've been pitching them on is, like, this kind of stuff, what I've done in the past pre-LLMs, was, like, you can feed this into your nurture tracks.
Like, you can do all sorts of fun stuff with this, with, like, on-demand webinars.
But, like, anything video-based you have, I think there's some pretty interesting.
We can play around and develop content off.
It's somewhat similar to what we're doing with them.
And then you link to the podcast, or in this case, not a podcast, but whatever video you have, and maybe do some fun stuff.
So just food for thought, as you all are, you know, I could see a customer story being some angle to explore, but not limited to that.
Yeah, this is super cool.
One question for you specifically on the podcast piece.
So Press and Podcast and Thought Leadership is a huge channel for us.
Our CEO is the one who really drives that.
How important is it that we, if we want to do something like this for like stuff that we hosted versus like he was a guest on?
So like, for instance, if you guys know Lenny Rizitzky, he's got probably like Rahul was just on the Lenny podcast and like, it's great.
And so I wonder if we would like actually want to leverage what he's doing with other channels and sort of like repost it to the blog and kind of do some stuff there.
Because we, I don't think we have.
An intention to set up our own podcast anytime soon, Morgan, Harmony, Ken.
No, I know.
I'm just kidding.
I'm trying to cut things off the list, not add to the list.
Oh, yeah.
You should be a fly on the wall in our conversation, Kyle.
I have, but yeah, I mean, I can count.
It's nice to be on the agency side of this after doing this for 10 plus years.
But anyways, yeah.
I mean, Rahul's doing like at least one of these a week.
And so like if that's something we want to try and plug into, then I think there's a clear opportunity there just because you were sharing podcasts.
But otherwise, let me just give you like the high level.
I just closed it to accident.
Quick answer is, yeah, you know, something to explore.

What's a little bit different in that process is they we can adapt workflow, but like there's ways we can if it is external, how do we extract the transcript from it in a different way and synthesize that?
But I'm sure we can figure that out.
At the end of the day, if you're a core focus or the topic is highly relevant.
For you all, which I imagine it would be, given that's why I'm speaking there, you know, that it's worth exploring for sure.
Cool.
Maybe I'll give you some examples of, like, the most recent high-impact ones that we can see if there's any good opportunities there.
Just to give you a sense real quick on, like, what our customer moments are going to look like, we've kind of broken this up into a few different things, too.
Like, our big rocks are, like, the marketing-driven campaigns and manufactured moments that we have, some of which are product-led.

We've also got kind of six product launches for the quarter, mostly along the AI and calendar themes with also this, like, analytics dashboard that we're launching.
Which is kind of an extension of some of the report type analytics and data.
there's like a lot I think happening here.
I imagine from our perspective, the AI announcements, the analytics dashboard maybe, and the calendar moments will have like product focused blog posts.
at least we'll have content and fodder that we could provide as like a starting point for how we might build our content too.
So we can get into the practice of like, hey, if it's a tier one launch, which is what the ones mean here, and we know we're going to do a blog post and whatnot.
Like how are we making sure that that gets shared over so that you guys could potentially like think through topics and content on top of that.
Yeah, very cool.
Yeah, there's probably ways to build clusters off of that in a variety of different ways or whatever that may look like.
You know, if you're creating a more product focused pillar page, there's different directions that could go.
But yeah, know, as these index towards oversharing with us as you continue to develop these plans and, you know, happy to continue.
Ideate on our end of where we can support.
Cool.
Yeah, Elvis, what's up?
Have you shared this with us?
Or no?
Not yet, because we're not quite finalized.
We are in the horse trading portion of the week, where we're trying to get the engineering team to commit to dates for all of these things.
that's always the most fun.
Yeah, yeah.
So my hope is that by the end of day tomorrow, we have the sequence locked out and committed to.
But I can start to share some of the stuff that we know is coming in April, if that helps.
We should, at some point, do a whole other deep dive on Calendar.
It's not that time-sensitive, I don't think, and I can kind of explain when we do it.
But that is an area that I think is worthwhile for us to through at some points, too, because it's an increasingly large area of engineering and product investment.
But, yeah, I can at least try to make sure that Emma...
My team shares the upcoming stuff for the AI campaign, because that's what she's really working on right now.
And then last thing I'll show you, too, is that Morgan and team are working on a couple of different webinars over the course of the quarter.
So we tend to do like one big webinar per month.
And so our April webinar is going to be focused on the AI inbox and like really driving engagement and understanding of how to use these new features.
In May, we plan to relaunch a Superhuman for Startups program, which is sort of how Superhuman is going to be geared up more to support smaller emerging startups.

And then in sort of along the same vein of this summer of AI campaign in June, we're going to do a like AI native productivity webinar where we're the vision is to get other like schools like us that are sort of AI first leaders in their category sort of come together and talk about productivity.
So hopefully some interesting partnership and stuff there.
So when this is done, I'll share it.
I also take this and actually put it into a calendar view when the engineering team commits to dates.
So I'll also make sure that you guys have that.
Very cool.
Exciting.
How is everyone's sanity now that you're getting towards the end of planning?
Feeling good?
Feeling excited?
Morgan survived her first planning week.
That's a, you know, who doesn't?
Not over until it's over, though.
You guys got this.
Hey, Alex, you wanted to talk about reporting?
Yeah, so sorry.
I know we got a little off track here.
Just on the last piece that was your topic, the next steps are you're going to set up some sort of page for us to share resources and we'll begin to sort of add to it.
Does that sound like a good point?
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
Okay, cool.
For reporting, then, I just wanted to see, like, it looks like...
Sharing, I think, Google Analytics, were those couple of screenshots there.
The expectation that you guys will set up some sort of dashboard for us where we can see impressions, clicks, performance by page.
We have some internal recording for blog, which I can show you.
I actually just pulled this up.
It's not so great.
So we could also maybe Harmony look on our end to like British or someone analytic to kind of help us build this into a bit of a better state.
But I was just curious what we should expect from GrowthX in terms of overall recording.
Yeah.
You can go ahead, Kyle.
I can add a little bit of context because this is even going to be, and Elvis didn't have a chance to be in part of this meeting where we're tweaking this a little bit.
But we leverage Looker Studio and typically are using Google Search Console and Google Analytics data to bring in there.
We are going to make a few modifications to our process to kind of enhance how that works a bit.
We

And so as we develop more content for you all, an example of how this can take shape is starting to break down the clusters in a way that helps us understand what's moving the needle, what's lagging, what are we learning from these different topical clusters.

The additional thing that we'll bake in there is what we see from AI search referrals, so the perplexities of the world, the chat GPTs, et cetera, because that is becoming more and more of a heavy part of our client's mix.
And at times, actually, if you go back to what I had shared earlier about the trajectory, we have one client, for example, who intentionally wanted to start a bit slower.
They're highly, highly technical content, you know, just really want to nail like a couple of pillar pieces.

And so their trajectory is naturally going to be a little bit slower, but what's been interesting has been that if we didn't look at the LOM referrals we were seeing, we actually would miss like quite a bit of value that's coming through for them, like highly engaged sessions from a variety.
Different sources.
And so we'll build in those different views that help us just look at the top level, we're seeing from like an organic search and what we see from some of those other referral sources.
And what are we learning from that?
It makes it a little bit of an interesting challenge of kind of like piecing those together a bit and telling a piece of story.
And then there's an additional angle, which sounds like maybe perhaps it's on your mind of like, how do we connect that down to like what you're seeing from conversions?
And so, you know, naturally that what that means for each one of our clients is going to be a bit different than how they attribute, what kind of data they can and cannot pull, where that data lives.
And so typically, you know, as we get to a phase where we can start to connect those dots, we have to consider different custom solutions and have to bring that together.
I've done this a million times myself and just, you know, and, you know, how to do this from an in-house perspective.
So depending on where you all are at and you mentioned it not being great, if there's anything we.
Help in making it better.
Happy to give some insight.
But yeah, we should explore how do we connect the dots so you can see the down funnel impact in some way or another.
Yeah, that sounds good.
I think we can own the conversion impact and how we're tracking it.
I mean, I'll just flash this up now too, so everyone sees it.
I think there's a little bit of an issue with us being able to share this.

I did check with our head of analytics on how we do it, but just basically tracks, and this is all time, so it's unfiltered, which is in and of itself not that helpful, but how many visitors have we had to the blog and what has that led to in terms of conversion?
then you can look at it on an individual page performance as well.
So do they convert before or after visit?
What is that conversion rate?
How many are still active?
And whatnot too.
So I think this deserves an overhaul of sorts.
And so I will make sure to get a request into our data science team to take a look at this at some point.
Point in Q2.
Yeah, again, who knows when we'll be able to submit to it.
But that, I think, can sort of fall on us.
But anything you can do to give us visibility into the, like, more top of funnel metrics, I think would be a full starting point.

Yeah, think is, you know, if we align where I found this to be as most help, while we can look at the individual charts and things like that, and how we want to be structured, if we first align on the questions we want to answer consistently, we can then figure out what systems need to be built to do that.
So we can collaborate on that.
And I find, as you're trying to evolve that dashboard you were looking at, that oftentimes makes it a little bit easier.
Yeah, with having some sort of segmentation, for example, by date, at the very least, would be helpful.
But yeah, you know, we'll start building out the Looker studio, we can share what that looks like.
And I'd say the main thing is just making sure, you know, we're accelerating publishing good content and
Bring that momentum.
That should be the number one right now, and then we can figure out what the data is telling us.
Sweet.
Sounds great.
Also, I don't know if you guys saw, I started using Superhuman, and it's pretty sweet, I'm not going to lie.
Love it.
You got to get the whole team on.
Yeah.
Harmony, got to bug Marcel.
I'm going to.
You guys are squarely in our ICP of professional services.
That rely on email, and I think it would be a huge value add.
I have not jumped in yet, and that's my bad, but, you know, I'm going to do it this week.
I'm going to do it this week.
You know what I noticed?
I'll tell you the first thing, like, on a gut level, what I noticed, like, the UI and the UX, it's so fast, and it makes you, like, it creates urgency for some reason.
Like, everything moves so fast.
You're like, oh, I got to, like, I got to figure, you know, I got to do this and get this out of the way.
Like, got to jam?
You got you.
You're not done until the E key on your keyboard exists no more, Elvis.
That's how you know.
It's so, like, I have it on my iPhone, and it's, like, it's so fast.
Like, when you hit a button, it's, like, it's super impressive.
Superhuman email.
Yeah.
Have you hit inbox zero yet?
I don't know what that, what does that mean?
Like, no emails?
Yeah, so, Harvey and I have this, it's really hard to get to inbox zero if you're not, like, used to the motion of doing that, but you have all these splits, right?
So I always hit inbox zero in my calendar split, which means that you have no, you mark everything done, and then you get these really cool images on your phone.
Look how fun this is.
Oh, I see.
I like it.
I dig it.
It feels really good to hit inbox zero.
I'm, like, trying to teach myself how to do it every day.
Alex is a pro at doing this, but.
It's a good habit building.
can do emails, though.
You need the emails.
Alex says he goes through it.
You need to give us all, like, yours.
A version of a training.
And he basically has reminders set up.
So if he's not going to respond to it right now and be done with it, then he just pushes it.
Yeah, it's the H key.
You just say, like, I don't need to deal with this today.
Remind me to do it tomorrow.
Remind me to do it on Monday.
The idea is, like, if you have nothing in your inbox, then, like, it's not a source of stress or distraction or anything like that.
So we have a bunch of really great content on this.
There's a team that helps enable folks.
They're actually doing internal trainings over the month of April, I think.
So Morgan, they're going do a way better job than I would.
That's for sure.

But when more of you all get on, too, we could organize, like, maybe a team training where one of our coaches will kind of come on with everyone on the team who is using it and kind of help walk you through some of our best practices.
And like I said, they're just, like, absolutely amazing.
I love it.
I don't want to care how many unreads I have in my email right now.
It's embarrassing.
I need to be better.
Cool.
All right, guys, we're...
Just out of time, we'll let you go.
All right, great catch-up.
Appreciate time, though.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Superhuman and GrowthX teams to discuss content strategy, reporting, and upcoming initiatives.](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX is ramping up content production, with 20+ articles ready for publication this week](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=102.0)
  - [Superhuman to share a repository of current visual assets (GIFs, images, videos) for use in content](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [Upcoming Superhuman initiatives include AI features launch, analytics dashboard, and webinars](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=751.0)
  - [GrowthX to set up Looker Studio dashboard for tracking content performance metrics](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=1054.0)

## Topics

### Content Production Progress

  - [First batch of content published last week](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=114.0)
  - [20+ more articles ready for publication this week](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=121.0)
  - [Early signs show good impression growth](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=143.0)
  - [Typical trajectory: 20-50 clicks/week by month 2, 200+ clicks/week by early month 3](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=204.0)

### Visual Asset Sharing

  - [GrowthX requested a file with Superhuman's key visual assets (GIFs, images, videos)](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)
  - [Alex to share GIF library from Dropbox, noting some design inconsistencies](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=391.0)
  - [YouTube videos available, including 1-minute product walkthroughs](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=380.0)
  - [Alex to compile best, most current assets in shared Notion doc](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=408.0)

### Upcoming Superhuman Initiatives

  - [AI features general availability launch in 2 weeks](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=751.0)
  - ["Summer of AI" campaign starting in July](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=761.0)
  - [6 product launches planned for the quarter, focusing on AI, calendar, and analytics dashboard](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=751.0)
  - [Monthly webinars: April (AI inbox), May (Superhuman for Startups), June (AI-native productivity)](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=915.0)

### Content Opportunities

  - [Potential to create SEO-rich articles from podcast/video transcripts](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=538.0)
  - [Explore leveraging Rahul's (CEO) frequent podcast guest appearances](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=649.0)
  - [Build content clusters around product launches and campaigns](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=751.0)

### Reporting and Analytics

  - [GrowthX to set up Looker Studio dashboard using Google Search Console and Analytics data](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=1054.0)
  - [Dashboard to include:](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=1054.0)
      - [Performance by page and topical clusters](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=1054.0)
      - [AI search referrals (e.g., Perplexity, ChatGPT)](https://fathom.video/share/RCD7-41xtHTn5Ba3FxqDQG97oKbUb__a?tab=summary&timestamp=1054.0)
